skill.name: amplitude-automation
skill.description: Automate Amplitude tasks via Rube MCP (Composio): events, user activity, cohorts, user identification. Always search tools first for current schemas.
skill.summary: Automate Amplitude analytics tasks via Rube MCP: send events, track user activity, manage cohorts, and identify users.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All 87 static findings are false positives. The skill is documentation for Amplitude API operations via Rube MCP. Backtick characters in markdown code blocks were misidentified as shell execution. URLs and API operations are legitimate product analytics functionality, not security risks.

content.value_statement: This skill enables Claude to automate Amplitude product analytics workflows including event tracking, user activity lookup, cohort management, and user identification through Rube MCP.
actual_capabilities: Send events to Amplitude for tracking user actions
Retrieve user activity and event history from Amplitude
Find and identify users by various identifiers
Manage cohorts: list, view details, update membership
Browse event categories to discover available event types
limitations: Requires Rube MCP server with active Amplitude connection
User ID resolution requires calling FIND_USER before activity lookup
Event timestamps must be in milliseconds (13-digit epoch)
Cohort membership updates are asynchronous and require status polling

use_cases.title: Track User Events
use_cases.description: Automatically send tracking events to Amplitude when users perform key actions in your application.
use_cases.target_user: Product managers and developers building analytics into apps

use_cases.title: Analyze User Behavior
use_cases.description: Look up user activity history to understand how users interact with your product.

use_cases.title: Manage User Segments
use_cases.description: Create and update cohort membership to segment users for targeted analysis or campaigns.

prompt_templates.title: Send Single Event
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the amplitude-automation skill to send a 'page_view' event to Amplitude with user_id 'user123'.

prompt_templates.title: Find User Activity
prompt_templates.prompt: Find user 'user456' in Amplitude, then get their recent activity.

prompt_templates.title: Update Cohort
prompt_templates.prompt: List the available cohorts, then add user 'user789' to the 'premium_users' cohort.

prompt_templates.title: Identify User Properties
prompt_templates.prompt: Set user properties for user 'user101' with plan='enterprise' and company='Acme Corp' using the IDENTIFY operation.

output_examples.input: Send a 'purchase' event for user 'user123' with event properties 'item': 'premium_plan', 'price': 99
output_examples.output: Event sent successfully to Amplitude. Event ID: evt_abc123. Data will be available in dashboards within minutes.

output_examples.input: Find user 'john@example.com' and get their last 10 events
output_examples.output: User found: Amplitude ID 'amp_xyz789'. Retrieved 10 events including 'page_view', 'button_click', 'form_submit' in reverse chronological order.

best_practices: Always call RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS first to get current tool schemas before use
Verify Amplitude connection status is ACTIVE before running workflows
Use batch events when sending multiple events to reduce API calls
Handle async operations like cohort updates with status polling
anti_patterns: Do not call GET_USER_ACTIVITY directly with application user IDs - must resolve to Amplitude internal ID first
Do not use seconds for timestamps - must use milliseconds (13-digit epoch)
Do not assume user property changes are immediate - they are eventually consistent

faq.question: What is Rube MCP?
faq.answer: Rube MCP is a Model Context Protocol server by Composio that provides integration with various tools including Amplitude analytics.

faq.question: Do I need an Amplitude API key?
faq.answer: No API key is required. The skill uses OAuth authentication through the Rube MCP connection flow.

faq.question: Why do I need to find the user first?
faq.answer: Amplitude uses internal user IDs separate from your application user IDs. FIND_USER resolves your user_id to Amplitude's internal ID required for activity queries.

faq.question: Why are my events not showing immediately?
faq.answer: Event ingestion is asynchronous. The API returns success but data takes a few minutes to become queryable in Amplitude dashboards.

faq.question: Can I update cohort membership in real-time?
faq.answer: Cohort membership updates are asynchronous. You must poll CHECK_COHORT_STATUS until the operation completes.

faq.question: What user property operations are available?
faq.answer: Amplitude IDENTIFY supports: $set (overwrite), $setOnce (set if not exists), $add (increment), $append (add to list), and $unset (remove).
